item.title: Nerve damage and a battery shortage: What's going on at Aston Martin?
item.description: Aston Martin's 2026 to this point nightmarish. Now it goes into the Australian GP unsure it will finish more than a few laps

item.title: Mercedes dominated Australia qualifying. Its rivals fear F1 2026 is already over
item.description: Mercedes' gap over the rest of the field in Australian GP qualifying have rivals fearing the season's contest is already over

item.title: Lance Stroll to race in F1 Australian Grand Prix despite failing to set qualifying lap time
item.description: Stroll failed to set a qualifying lap time, and Aston Martin needed to request for the stewards to allow him to start Sunday’s F1 race.

item.title: Max Verstappen escapes injury in Australian GP qualifying crash
item.description: Max Verstappen had to be sent to have his hands x-ray after his crash in 2026 F1 Australian GP qualifying

item.title: Qualifying recap from the 2026 F1 Australian Grand Prix
item.description: George Russell swept to the first pole of the 2026 Formula 1 season with a dominant display for Mercedes in Australian GP qualifying

item.title: 500 races for the F1 driver you never see. Plus: 30 years of F1 in Melbourne
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Oscar Piastri is carrying 45 years of Australian F1 hope. This time, he's brought help
item.description: Australia took Piastri's title loss to parliament. He took it to his mindset coach

item.title: F1 stewards to be more lenient over clashes between drivers in 2026 under expanded racing guidelines
item.description: The FIA's guideline changes cover “racing to the apex” driving and how drivers cannot simply disappear once they have been overtaken.

item.title: With Iranian air strikes near its circuits, F1's Middle East races are in real doubt
item.description: Like many, F1 staff are anxiously watching the current Middle East conflict. Decisions on the 2026 races occurring there soon must be taken

item.title: Aston Martin F1 crisis deepens with engine battery shortage at 2026 Australian GP
item.description: Aston Martin team boss Adrian Newey has revealed just how short the F1 team is running on critical engine parts ahead of the first 2026 race

item.title: Australian GP track breakdown: F1's new era begins in Melbourne
item.description: The layout of this F1 track is true to its name: a high-speed rush around a park.

item.title: After a nightmare season, Lewis Hamilton is putting himself back together
item.description: Hamilton has outlined recovering from a tough first season with Ferrari to be better this year: "You won't see that person again"

item.title: F1 season predictions: Our picks for 2026's title race, surprises and one 'dumpster fire'
item.description: From champions to best of the rest, our staff is ready to lock in their F1 forecasts as a new era begins

item.title: Aston Martin F1 drivers at risk of nerve damage from car vibrations, will have laps capped in Australian GP
item.description: Aston Martin will retire early from the Australian Grand Prix due to an unsafe vibration in the car

item.title: Daniel Ricciardo's mailbag: Ex-F1 driver answers your questions
item.description: Ex-F1 driver and Australian racer Daniel Ricciardo exclusively answers fan questions on the eve of the 2026 Australian GP

item.title: How F1 finally put its cars on a diet: 'It doesn't feel like such a big bus'
item.description: F1's 2026 cars are lighter, smaller, and sharper. But will this make the racing better?

item.title: My doubts ahead of the season opener. Plus: Leclerc's dog Leo, very good boy
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: The 2026 F1 season was supposed to be Aston Martin's moment. It faces embarrassment instead
item.description: Aston Martin was supposed to be an F1 title contender in 2026. Instead, it may barely race in Australia. Here's what has gone so wrong

item.title: Ferrari's stunning starts will be a key F1 2026 advantage. And its rivals can't copy
item.description: The F1 pecking order remains shrouded in mystery ahead of the 2026 season. But the Ferrari team seems to have an edge its rivals can't copy

item.title: F1 chartering flights to ensure key staff arrive at Australian Grand Prix on time
item.description: The fallout from the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran on Saturday is disrupting travel in the Middle East, a major global transport hub.

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Formula 1 monitoring Middle East conflict after Iran strikes U.S. base near Bahrain circuit
item.description: F1's new season visits Bahrain and Saudi Arabia in six weeks

item.title: F1 manufacturers agree to engine testing rule change after compression ratio row
item.description: In December, it emerged that Mercedes had found a possible loophole in the rules that could lead to a performance advantage.

item.title: The first time Alonso-Honda went wrong. Plus: How you rank 2026 car liveries
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic’s F1 newsletter.

item.title: McLaren agrees settlement with Chip Ganassi Racing over Alex Palou’s contract breach
item.description: McLaren won the case it brought against Palou following his decision to renege on his agreement to join its IndyCar team in 2023.

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: What is F1’s DRS replacement, and how is it different from IndyCar’s push-to-pass?
item.description: F1's incoming Manual Override Mode looks a lot like IndyCar's overtaking aid, with a few key differences.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
